mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market slack that reduces wage inflation pressures and corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and capital demand, forcing earnings downgrades and negative market returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% month-over-month signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that trigger inflation fears and negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth consistently coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate confirms a restrictive monetary stance that suppresses risk appetite and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between inflation and rates shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenues and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized indicates economic stagnation that triggers recession risk premiums and immediate negative market repricing.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of quarters where GDP growth drops below the 1.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stagnant employment signals capital-intensive automation or productivity shocks that disrupt sectoral earnings and cause negative market volatility.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with a hit rate above 55% in walk-forward validation.
